contracts/ITeamProofVerifier.sol.content:
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
pragma solidity ^0.8.20;

/**
 * @title ITeamProofVerifier
 * @author VAPI Project
 * @notice Interface for the auto-generated snarkjs Groth16 verifier contract.
 *
 * @dev The concrete implementation is generated by:
 *        cd contracts/circuits && bash setup.sh
 *      which produces contracts/contracts/TeamProofVerifier.sol via:
 *        npx snarkjs zkey export solidityverifier TeamProof_final.zkey TeamProofVerifier.sol
 *
 *      Circuit public inputs (must match contracts/circuits/TeamProof.circom order):
 *        [0] poseidonMerkleRoot  — Poseidon tree root of 8-leaf commitment tree
 *        [1] nullifierHash       — Poseidon(poseidonMerkleRoot, identitySecrets[0], epoch)
 *        [2] memberCount         — Active member count in [2, 6]
 *        [3] epoch               — block.number / EPOCH_BLOCKS (anti-replay domain tag)
 *
 *      Proof wire format (256 bytes, ABI-packed):
 *        bytes[0:64]    a   — G1 point (pi_a.x, pi_a.y)
 *        bytes[64:192]  b   — G2 point (pi_b[0][0], pi_b[0][1], pi_b[1][0], pi_b[1][1])
 *        bytes[192:256] c   — G1 point (pi_c.x, pi_c.y)
 *
 *      Decode via: abi.decode(proof, (uint256[2], uint256[2][2], uint256[2]))
 */
interface ITeamProofVerifier {
    /**
     * @notice Verify a Groth16 BN254 proof against the TeamProof circuit.
     *
     * @param a      G1 proof component (pi_a): [x, y]
     * @param b      G2 proof component (pi_b): [[x0, x1], [y0, y1]]
     * @param c      G1 proof component (pi_c): [x, y]
     * @param input  Public inputs in circuit declaration order:
     *                 input[0] = poseidonMerkleRoot
     *                 input[1] = nullifierHash (as uint256)
     *                 input[2] = memberCount
     *                 input[3] = epoch
     * @return       True if the proof is valid.
     */
    function verifyProof(
        uint256[2] memory a,
        uint256[2][2] memory b,
        uint256[2] memory c,
        uint256[4] memory input
    ) external view returns (bool);
}

@dev Contract module which provides a basic access control mechanism, where
 there is an account (an owner) that can be granted exclusive access to
 specific functions.
 The initial owner is set to the address provided by the deployer. This can
 later be changed with {transferOwnership}.
 This module is used through inheritance. It will make available the modifier
 `onlyOwner`, which can be applied to your functions to restrict their use to
 the owner.
@dev The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation.
@dev The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)
@dev Initializes the contract setting the address provided by the deployer as the initial owner.
@dev Throws if called by any account other than the owner.
@dev Returns the address of the current owner.
@dev Throws if the sender is not the owner.

@dev Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call
 `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner.
 NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner,
 thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

@dev Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`).
 Can only be called by the current owner.

@dev Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`).
 Internal function without access restriction.

@dev Provides information about the current execution context, including the
 sender of the transaction and its data. While these are generally available
 via msg.sender and msg.data, they should not be accessed in such a direct
 manner, since when dealing with meta-transactions the account sending and
 paying for execution may not be the actual sender (as far as an application
 is concerned).
 This contract is only required for intermediate, library-like contracts.

@dev Contract module that helps prevent reentrant calls to a function.
 Inheriting from `ReentrancyGuard` will make the {nonReentrant} modifier
 available, which can be applied to functions to make sure there are no nested
 (reentrant) calls to them.
 Note that because there is a single `nonReentrant` guard, functions marked as
 `nonReentrant` may not call one another. This can be worked around by making
 those functions `private`, and then adding `external` `nonReentrant` entry
 points to them.
 TIP: If EIP-1153 (transient storage) is available on the chain you're deploying at,
 consider using {ReentrancyGuardTransient} instead.
 TIP: If you would like to learn more about reentrancy and alternative ways
 to protect against it, check out our blog post
 https://blog.openzeppelin.com/reentrancy-after-istanbul/[Reentrancy After Istanbul].
@dev Unauthorized reentrant call.

@dev Prevents a contract from calling itself, directly or indirectly.
 Calling a `nonReentrant` function from another `nonReentrant`
 function is not supported. It is possible to prevent this from happening
 by making the `nonReentrant` function external, and making it call a
 `private` function that does the actual work.

@dev Returns true if the reentrancy guard is currently set to "entered", which indicates there is a
 `nonReentrant` function in the call stack.

@notice Minimum registration deposit in wei (anti-Sybil).
@notice Cooldown period after deactivation before deposit can be withdrawn.
@notice Maximum value for the reputation score (basis-point scale, 10000 = 100%).
@notice deviceId => DeviceInfo. deviceId = keccak256(pubkey).
@notice Addresses authorized to call updateReputation (e.g. PoACVerifier, BountyMarket).

@param _minimumDeposit Initial minimum deposit in wei for device registration.

 @param _newMinimum New minimum deposit in wei.

 @param _deviceId The deactivated device whose deposit to withdraw.

@dev Internal reputation computation.
      Formula (simple linear model with diminishing returns on volume):
        - verifiedBase  = min(verifiedCount, 10000) (cap raw PoAC contribution)
        - corrobBonus   = corroborations * 2
        - disputePenalty = disputes * 10
        - rawScore = verifiedBase + corrobBonus - disputePenalty
        - Normalize into [0, MAX_REPUTATION] using a logistic-style clamp:
          score = rawScore * MAX_REPUTATION / (rawScore + 1000)
          (Approaches MAX_REPUTATION asymptotically as rawScore grows)
        - If rawScore <= 0, score = 0.

@title PoACVerifier
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice On-chain verification of Proof of Autonomous Cognition (PoAC) records
         submitted by VAPI devices on the IoTeX blockchain.
 @dev Verification accepts the raw 164-byte body exactly as signed by the
      firmware (no re-serialization), plus the 64-byte ECDSA-P256 signature.
      Pipeline:
        1. Validate body/signature lengths (164 / 64 bytes).
        2. Check device is registered and active in {DeviceRegistry}.
        3. Compute SHA-256 of the raw body (identical to firmware digest).
        4. Verify the ECDSA-P256 signature over that SHA-256 digest
           using the IoTeX P256 precompile at address 0x0100.
        5. Parse fields from the raw body for semantic validation.
        6. Validate monotonic counter, timestamp skew, chain linkage.
        7. Store SHA-256(body) as the chain head and record hash.
           This matches the firmware's chain_head = SHA-256(serialized_body),
           ensuring prev_poac_hash linkage is consistent end-to-end.
        8. Update the device's reputation in {DeviceRegistry}.
      Raw body layout (big-endian, matching firmware poac_serialize()):
        Offset  Field                  Size
        0x00    prev_poac_hash         32B
        0x20    sensor_commitment      32B
        0x40    model_manifest_hash    32B
        0x60    world_model_hash       32B
        0x80    inference_result       1B
        0x81    action_code            1B
        0x82    confidence             1B
        0x83    battery_pct            1B
        0x84    monotonic_ctr          4B   (uint32 big-endian)
        0x88    timestamp_ms           8B   (int64 big-endian)
        0x90    latitude               8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)
        0x98    longitude              8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)
        0xA0    bounty_id              4B   (uint32 big-endian)
        ---     total                  164B
      ## IoTeX P256 Precompile (0x0100)
      Input:  hash(32) || r(32) || s(32) || x(32) || y(32) = 160 bytes
      Output: 0x01 (32-byte left-padded) if valid, 0x00 otherwise.
@notice IoTeX P256 signature verification precompile address.
@notice Size of the serialized PoAC body (before signature).
@notice Size of the ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s).
@notice Maximum allowable timestamp skew (seconds).
@notice Chain tracking state for each device.
@notice Reference to the DeviceRegistry contract.
@notice Per-device chain integrity state. deviceId => ChainState.
@notice Record hash => true if this PoAC has been verified on-chain.
@notice Total number of PoAC records verified across all devices.

@notice Sensor commitment schema version for records verified via verifyPoACWithSchema().
         0 = not set / unknown (record submitted via legacy verifyPoAC()).
         1 = v1 environmental (Pebble Tracker: temp/humidity/pressure/GPS/accel).
         2 = v2 kinematic/haptic (DualShock: sticks/triggers/gyro/accel + adaptive trigger).
@notice Emitted when a PoAC record is successfully verified.
@notice Emitted when a batch verification completes.

@param _deviceRegistry   Address of the deployed DeviceRegistry contract.
 @param _maxTimestampSkew  Maximum acceptable timestamp skew in seconds.
@notice External wrapper for try/catch in batch. Not for direct use.
@dev Parsed fields from the raw body, packed into a struct to avoid stack-too-deep.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (bytes calldata) pure returns (struct PoACVerifier.ParsedFields memory)
function (bytes32) view external returns (struct DeviceRegistry.DeviceInfo memory)

documentation.text:
@title TeamProofAggregator
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice Swarm-based collaborative gaming with verifiable team proofs.
 @dev Aggregates individual PoAC record hashes from team members into a
      compact Merkle root that serves as a team-level attestation.
      Use cases:
        - Esports tournament integrity: all team members verified clean
        - Cooperative game achievements: verifiable team contribution
        - DAO governance: team-weighted voting based on collective proof
      Merkle construction:
        1. Sort individual record hashes lexicographically
        2. Compute pairwise keccak256 hashes up the tree
        3. Odd leaf is promoted unchanged
        4. Root = team attestation hash
      Integrity guarantees:
        - Every member's record must be verified in PoACVerifier
        - No member can have cheat flags (inference in [0x22, 0x29])
        - All members must belong to the registered team
@notice Global proof counter (also serves as proof ID).
@notice proofId => TeamProof
@notice teamId => array of proof IDs

@notice Thrown when a team member's record contains a cheat-flag inference code.
         No team proof may contain records flagged by the VAPI PITL anti-cheat layer.

@dev Compute Merkle root from an array of leaf hashes.
      Sorts leaves lexicographically, then hashes pairwise.
@dev Insertion sort for small arrays (max 6 elements).
@notice Verify a Merkle root against individual record hashes (off-chain helper).

@title TeamProofAggregatorZK
 @author VAPI Project
 @notice Extends TeamProofAggregator with a ZK proof gate for privacy-preserving
         team attestation.
 @dev Architecture (Phase 14C):
      - submitTeamProofZK() (5-param) — Phase 13 backward-compat; mock proof path.
      - submitTeamProofZK() (7-param) — Phase 14C real path; requires poseidonMerkleRoot
        and epoch, which the Python ZKProver (bridge/zk_prover.py) provides.
      - _verifyZKProof() is virtual — upgraded to real Groth16 when teamProofVerifier
        is set to the address of the auto-generated TeamProofVerifier.sol contract.
      - usedNullifiers prevents replay: each nullifierHash may only be used once.
      Two-mode operation:
        Mock path (teamProofVerifier == address(0) OR poseidonMerkleRoot == 0):
          _verifyZKProof returns true iff proof.length == MOCK_PROOF_SIZE (256 bytes).
          Compatible with all Phase 13 tests and the Phase 14C mock fallback in
          bridge/zk_prover.py.
        Real path (teamProofVerifier set, poseidonMerkleRoot != 0):
          Decodes the 256-byte ABI-packed proof into (G1, G2, G1) components.
          Calls ITeamProofVerifier.verifyProof() with the decoded components and
          four public inputs: [poseidonMerkleRoot, nullifierHash, memberCount, epoch].
 ZK Circuit: contracts/circuits/TeamProof.circom
 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   Public inputs (order matches circuit declaration):
     poseidonMerkleRoot : uint256  — Poseidon tree root of 8-leaf commitment tree
     nullifierHash      : uint256  — Poseidon(poseidonMerkleRoot, identitySecrets[0], epoch)
     memberCount        : uint256  — Active members in [2, 6]
     epoch              : uint256  — block.number / EPOCH_BLOCKS (anti-replay)
   Private inputs:
     inferenceResults[6] — 8-bit VAPI inference codes (secret; must not be in [40, 42])
     identitySecrets[6]  — Poseidon(deviceId) per member
     activeFlags[6]      — 1 = active slot; sum == memberCount
   Proof wire format (256 bytes; abi.decode compatible):
     [0:64]    a   — G1 point (pi_a.x, pi_a.y)
     [64:192]  b   — G2 point (pi_b[0][0..1], pi_b[1][0..1])
     [192:256] c   — G1 point (pi_c.x, pi_c.y)
 Setup:
   1. cd contracts/circuits && npm install && bash setup.sh
   2. Deploy TeamProofVerifier.sol (auto-generated at contracts/contracts/TeamProofVerifier.sol)
   3. Call setTeamProofVerifier(<TeamProofVerifier address>)
   4. Bridge: set VAPI_ZK_WASM_PATH / VAPI_ZK_ZKEY_PATH / VAPI_ZK_VKEY_PATH
   5. Submit proofs via 7-param submitTeamProofZK()
@notice Expected proof byte length (Phase 13 mock + Phase 14C real Groth16).
@notice Tracks used nullifiers to prevent ZK proof replay.
@notice Emitted when the Groth16 verifier contract address is updated.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (uint256[2] memory,uint256[2] memory[2] memory,uint256[2] memory,uint256[4] memory) view external returns (bool)

documentation.text: @notice Set the mock ZK verification result. Call with false to simulate invalid proof.

Project","details":"Each device is identified by a `deviceId` derived as keccak256 of its      uncompressed SEC1 P256 public key (65 bytes: 0x04 || x || y).      IoTeX supports ECDSA-P256 signature verification via a precompile at      address 0x0100. The EVM does not natively support P256, so all on-chain      signature checks MUST use this precompile. See {PoACVerifier} for usage.      Registration requires a deposit to prevent Sybil attacks. The deposit is      held for the lifetime of the device registration and can be reclaimed      upon deactivation after a cooldown period. {"compiler":{"version":"0.8.20+commit.a1b79de6"},"language":"Solidity","output":{"abi":[{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint256[2]","name":"a","type":"uint256[2]"},{"internalType":"uint256[2][2]","name":"b","type":"uint256[2][2]"},{"internalType":"uint256[2]","name":"c","type":"uint256[2]"},{"internalType":"uint256[4]","name":"input","type":"uint256[4]"}],"name":"verifyProof","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"author":"VAPI Project","details":"The concrete implementation is generated by:        cd contracts/circuits && bash setup.sh      which produces contracts/contracts/TeamProofVerifier.sol via:        npx snarkjs zkey export solidityverifier TeamProof_final.zkey TeamProofVerifier.sol      Circuit public inputs (must match contracts/circuits/TeamProof.circom order):        [0] poseidonMerkleRoot  \u2014 Poseidon tree root of 8-leaf commitment tree        [1] nullifierHash       \u2014 Poseidon(poseidonMerkleRoot, identitySecrets[0], epoch)        [2] memberCount         \u2014 Active member count in [2, 6]        [3] epoch               \u2014 block.number / EPOCH_BLOCKS (anti-replay domain tag)      Proof wire format (256 bytes, ABI-packed):        bytes[0:64]    a   \u2014 G1 point (pi_a.x, pi_a.y)        bytes[64:192]  b   \u2014 G2 point (pi_b[0][0], pi_b[0][1], pi_b[1][0], pi_b[1][1])        bytes[192:256] c   \u2014 G1 point (pi_c.x, pi_c.y)      Decode via: abi.decode(proof, (uint256[2], uint256[2][2], uint256[2]))","kind":"dev","methods":{"verifyProof(uint256[2],uint256[2][2],uint256[2],uint256[4])":{"params":{"a":"G1 proof component (pi_a): [x, y]","b":"G2 proof component (pi_b): [[x0, x1], [y0, y1]]","c":"G1 proof component (pi_c): [x, y]","input":"Public inputs in circuit declaration order:                 input[0] = poseidonMerkleRoot                 input[1] = nullifierHash (as uint256)                 input[2] = memberCount                 input[3] = epoch"},"returns":{"_0":"True if the proof is valid."}}},"title":"ITeamProofVerifier","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"verifyProof(uint256[2],uint256[2][2],uint256[2],uint256[4])":{"notice":"Verify a Groth16 BN254 proof against the TeamProof circuit."}},"notice":"Interface for the auto-generated snarkjs Groth16 verifier Project","details":"Verification accepts the raw 164-byte body exactly as signed by the      firmware (no re-serialization), plus the 64-byte ECDSA-P256 signature.      Pipeline:        1. Validate body/signature lengths (164 / 64 bytes).        2. Check device is registered and active in {DeviceRegistry}.        3. Compute SHA-256 of the raw body (identical to firmware digest).        4. Verify the ECDSA-P256 signature over that SHA-256 digest           using the IoTeX P256 precompile at address 0x0100.        5. Parse fields from the raw body for semantic validation.        6. Validate monotonic counter, timestamp skew, chain linkage.        7. Store SHA-256(body) as the chain head and record hash.           This matches the firmware's chain_head = SHA-256(serialized_body),           ensuring prev_poac_hash linkage is consistent end-to-end.        8. Update the device's reputation in {DeviceRegistry}.      Raw body layout (big-endian, matching firmware poac_serialize()):        Offset  Field                  Size        0x00    prev_poac_hash         32B        0x20    sensor_commitment      32B        0x40    model_manifest_hash    32B        0x60    world_model_hash       32B        0x80    inference_result       1B        0x81    action_code            1B        0x82    confidence             1B        0x83    battery_pct            1B        0x84    monotonic_ctr          4B   (uint32 big-endian)        0x88    timestamp_ms           8B   (int64 big-endian)        0x90    latitude               8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)        0x98    longitude              8B   (IEEE 754 double big-endian)        0xA0    bounty_id              4B   (uint32 big-endian)        ---     total                  164B      ## IoTeX P256 Precompile (0x0100)      Input:  hash(32) || r(32) || s(32) || x(32) || y(32) = 160 bytes      Output: 0x01 (32-byte left-padded) if valid, 0x00 otherwise.","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"constructor":{"params":{"_deviceRegistry":"Address of the deployed DeviceRegistry contract.","_maxTimestampSkew":"Maximum acceptable timestamp skew in seconds."}},"getRecordSchema(bytes32)":{"params":{"_recordHash":"SHA-256 hash of the PoAC body."},"returns":{"isSet":"        False when record was verified via legacy verifyPoAC() call.","schemaVersion":"1 = v1 environmental, 2 = v2 kinematic. 0 if not set."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner."},"verifyPoAC(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"params":{"_deviceId":"Device identifier (keccak256 of device pubkey).","_rawBody":"The 164-byte serialized PoAC body (exactly as signed).","_signature":"ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s, 64 bytes)."},"returns":{"recordHash":"SHA-256 of the body (matches firmware chain head)."}},"verifyPoACBatch(bytes32[],bytes[],bytes[])":{"details":"Records that fail are silently skipped (not reverted).      The BatchVerified event reports counts."},"verifyPoACWithSchema(bytes32,bytes,bytes,uint8)":{"details":"Identical to verifyPoAC() but additionally persists the schema version so that         downstream contracts (ProgressAttestation) can enforce schema compatibility.","params":{"_deviceId":"Device identifier (keccak256 of device pubkey).","_rawBody":"The 164-byte serialized PoAC body (exactly as signed).","_schemaVersion":"Sensor schema: 1 = v1 environmental, 2 = v2 kinematic. Must be > 0.","_signature":"ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s, 64 bytes)."},"returns":{"recordHash":"   SHA-256 of the body (matches firmware chain head)."}}},"stateVariables":{"submittedHashes":{"details":"Unlike {verifiedRecords} (set only on full verification success),         submittedHashes is set in the OUTER batch context so it persists even         when the child verification call reverts, permanently preventing         re-submission of any body that entered a batch \u2014 regardless of outcome."}},"title":"PoACVerifier","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"BatchVerified(uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a batch verification completes."},"PoACVerified(bytes32,bytes32,uint32,int64,uint8,uint8,uint32)":{"notice":"Emitted when a PoAC record is successfully verified."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"P256_PRECOMPILE()":{"notice":"IoTeX P256 signature verification precompile address."},"POAC_BODY_SIZE()":{"notice":"Size of the serialized PoAC body (before signature)."},"POAC_SIG_SIZE()":{"notice":"Size of the ECDSA-P256 signature (r || s)."},"deviceRegistry()":{"notice":"Reference to the DeviceRegistry contract."},"getRecordSchema(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns the sensor commitment schema version for a verified record."},"maxTimestampSkew()":{"notice":"Maximum allowable timestamp skew (seconds)."},"recordHasSchema(bytes32)":{"notice":"True if a schema version was explicitly provided for this record hash.         Distinguishes \"schema not set\" (false) from \"schema 0 explicitly set\" (impossible \u2014         verifyPoACWithSchema rejects schemaVersion==0)."},"recordInferences(bytes32)":{"notice":"Inference result extracted from each verified PoAC body at byte offset 0x80.         Key insight: _parseBody() already parses this; we persist it for downstream queries.         Default 0 for unverified records (cheat codes start at 0x28, so 0 is always clean)."},"recordSchemas(bytes32)":{"notice":"Sensor commitment schema version for records verified via verifyPoACWithSchema().         0 = not set / unknown (record submitted via legacy verifyPoAC()).         1 = v1 environmental (Pebble Tracker: temp/humidity/pressure/GPS/accel).         2 = v2 kinematic/haptic (DualShock: sticks/triggers/gyro/accel + adaptive trigger)."},"submittedHashes(bytes32)":{"notice":"Record hash => true if this body was ever submitted to this contract."},"totalVerifiedCount()":{"notice":"Total number of PoAC records verified across all devices."},"verifiedRecords(bytes32)":{"notice":"Record hash => true if this PoAC has been verified on-chain."},"verifyPoAC(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"notice":"Verify a single PoAC record from its raw wire-format bytes."},"verifyPoACBatch(bytes32[],bytes[],bytes[])":{"notice":"Batch-verify multiple PoAC records."},"verifyPoACExternal(bytes32,bytes,bytes)":{"notice":"External wrapper for try/catch in batch. Project","details":"Aggregates individual PoAC record hashes from team members into a      compact Merkle root that serves as a team-level attestation.      Use cases:        - Esports tournament integrity: all team members verified clean        - Cooperative game achievements: verifiable team contribution        - DAO governance: team-weighted voting based on collective proof      Merkle construction:        1. Sort individual record hashes lexicographically        2. Compute pairwise keccak256 hashes up the tree        3. Odd leaf is promoted unchanged        4. Root = team attestation hash      Integrity guarantees:        - Every member's record must be verified in PoACVerifier        - No member can have cheat flags (inference in [0x22, 0x29])        - All members must belong to the registered team","errors":{"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. (eg. `address(0)`)"}],"OwnableUnauthorizedAccount(address)":[{"details":"The caller account is not authorized to perform an operation."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"createTeam(bytes32,bytes32[])":{"params":{"_deviceIds":"Array of member device IDs (2-6 members).","_teamId":"Unique team identifier (e.g., keccak256 of team name)."}},"owner()":{"details":"Returns the address of the current owner."},"renounceOwnership()":{"details":"Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner."},"submitTeamProof(bytes32,bytes32[],bytes32)":{"params":{"_merkleRoot":"Pre-computed Merkle root of sorted record hashes.","_recordHashes":"One verified PoAC record hash per team member (in member order).","_teamId":"Team identifier."},"returns":{"proofId":"      Unique proof identifier."}},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner."}},"stateVariables":{"CHEAT_INFERENCE_MIN":{"details":"0x28 = DRIVER_INJECT, 0x29 = WALLHACK_PREAIM, 0x2A = AIMBOT_BEHAVIORAL.         Any record with inferenceResult in [CHEAT_INFERENCE_MIN, CHEAT_INFERENCE_MAX]         is rejected by submitTeamProof \u2014 esports integrity guarantee."}},"title":"TeamProofAggregator","version":1},"userdoc":{"errors":{"CheatFlagDetected(bytes32,uint8)":[{"notice":"Thrown when a team member's record contains a cheat-flag inference code.         No team proof may contain records flagged by the VAPI PITL anti-cheat layer."}]},"kind":"user","methods":{"CHEAT_INFERENCE_MIN()":{"notice":"Inclusive range of cheat-flag inference result codes (Phase 8 PITL)."},"computeMerkleRoot(bytes32[])":{"notice":"Verify a Merkle root against individual record hashes (off-chain helper)."},"createTeam(bytes32,bytes32[])":{"notice":"Register a new team with its member device IDs."},"proofCount()":{"notice":"Global proof counter (also serves as proof ID)."},"submitTeamProof(bytes32,bytes32[],bytes32)":{"notice":"Submit a team proof aggregating individual PoAC is set to the address of the auto-generated TeamProofVerifier.sol contract.      - usedNullifiers prevents replay: each nullifierHash may only be used once.      Two-mode operation:        Mock path (teamProofVerifier == address(0) OR poseidonMerkleRoot == 0):          _verifyZKProof returns true iff proof.length == MOCK_PROOF_SIZE (256 bytes).          Compatible with all Phase 13 tests and the Phase 14C mock fallback in          bridge/zk_prover.py.        Real path (teamProofVerifier set, poseidonMerkleRoot != 0):          Decodes the 256-byte ABI-packed proof into (G1, G2, G1) components.          Calls ITeamProofVerifier.verifyProof() with the decoded components and          four public inputs: [poseidonMerkleRoot, nullifierHash, memberCount, epoch].
